text.title: AWS Networking Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS Networking` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: VPC MUST have at minimum public + private + data subnets per AZ; workloads MUST go to private; only ALBs / NAT / VPN go to public.

rationale.source: AWS VPC Design Best Practices; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Single-tier VPCs expose workloads to ingress directly; tiered subnets are the only defensible default.

rule.statement: Production VPC MUST span ≥ 2 AZs; data-plane services (RDS, ElastiCache) MUST be Multi-AZ.
rule.rationale: Single-AZ services have ~99.9% SLA; Multi-AZ raises to ~99.99%.

rule.statement: VPC endpoints (Gateway for S3 / DynamoDB; Interface for the rest) MUST be deployed for AWS services used from private subnets. NAT-only egress to AWS is forbidden where an endpoint exists.

rationale.source: AWS VPC Endpoints docs; FinOps reports

rule.rationale: NAT egress to AWS services is needless cost + adds public-internet hop; endpoints are cheaper + private.

rule.statement: Security groups MUST NOT have inbound 0.0.0.0/0 except for known public listeners (ALB on 443, CloudFront origin). All other ingress = explicit CIDR / SG reference.

rationale.source: AWS Security Group Best Practices; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: 0.0.0.0/0 ingress is the most common over-permissive SG class; explicit scoping bounds attack surface.

rule.statement: VPC Flow Logs MUST be enabled at VPC level; logs sent to CloudWatch / S3 with ≥ 90d retention. Per-ENI flow logs forbidden as primary (use VPC-level).

rationale.source: AWS VPC Flow Logs docs; SOC2

rule.rationale: Without flow logs, network forensics post-incident is impossible; VPC-level is the cheapest forensic surface.

rule.statement: Production NAT MUST be NAT Gateway (managed), Multi-AZ (one per AZ), with route tables pinned to same-AZ. NAT instance forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: Self-managed NAT instances are a single-point-of-failure + scaling bottleneck; NAT Gateway is managed + zonal.

rule.statement: Multi-account / multi-VPC connectivity MUST use Transit Gateway in a network account; full-mesh VPC peering forbidden beyond 3 VPCs.

rationale.source: AWS Transit Gateway docs; LZ Accelerator

rule.rationale: VPC peering scales O(n²); TGW gives O(n) and centralises routing + inspection.

rule.statement: VPC CIDR + IPv6 plan MUST be defined upfront and registered in IPAM (AWS IPAM or equivalent); ad-hoc /16 grabs forbidden.
rule.rationale: Random CIDRs cause RFC1918 collisions at scale; IPAM prevents the inevitable peering conflicts.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
